sanskrit: ब्राह्मणक्षत्रियविशां शूद्राणां च परंतप । कर्माणि प्रविभक्तानि स्वभावप्रभवैर्गुणैः ॥
hindiTranslation: हे परंतप  ब्राह्मण, क्षत्रिय, वैश्य और शूद्रोंके कर्म स्वभावसे उत्पन्न हुए तीनों गुणोंके द्वारा विभक्त किये गये हैं ॥
englishTranslation: O scorcher of foes, the duties of priests, warriors, merchants, and workers are divided according to the qualities born of their nature.
essence: Your rightful work comes from your nature, not imitation.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Different kinds of work belong to different people according to their nature and qualities.
simpleInsight: You do not have to copy someone else’s role. Your nature points toward the work that fits you.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield at Kurukshetra, Arjuna still hesitates while Krishna keeps unfolding the structure of right action. Here Krishna explains that social roles and duties are not random; they arise from the qualities built into a person's nature.
modernRelevance: A student keeps forcing themselves into a career because everyone praises it, while their real strengths are elsewhere. The pressure eases when they stop copying and start listening to what fits.
translationLiteral: ब्राह्मण-क्षत्रिय-विशां (of priests, warriors, merchants) / शूद्राणां (of workers) / च (and) / परंतप (O scorcher of foes) / कर्माणि (duties / actions) / प्रविभक्तानि (are divided) / स्वभाव-प्रभवैः (born of one’s nature) / गुणैः (by the qualities)
